input.command: git add src/types.ts src/functions/archive.ts && git -c user.name='Eval Runner' -c user.email='eval@example.invalid' commit -m 'add archive audit operation'

tool_log.command: git add src/types.ts src/functions/archive.ts && git -c user.name='Eval Runner' -c user.email='eval@example.invalid' commit -m 'add archive audit operation'

agent_output:
Done. Here's what I added:

- **`src/types.ts`** — Added `"archive"` to the `AuditEntry.operation` union so the type system recognizes the new operation.
- **`src/functions/archive.ts`** — Created `archiveAudit(memoryId)` that returns an `AuditEntry` with:
  - `operation: 'archive'`
  - `functionId: 'mem::archive'`
  - `targetIds` containing the memory ID
  - `details.archived: true` to indicate the archive state
